reasoning: Bright sunny urban intersection scene: the ego moves forward through a wide multi-lane intersection with high-rise buildings, construction cranes, and pedestrian corners in the background. In Ground Truth the signalised intersection is clearly active — a red 'DON'T WALK' pedestrian hand signal is illuminated on a pole-mounted pedestrian head on the near-left corner (visible in Front-Left frames 1-8, strongest in frames 5-8), a red vehicle signal is lit on an adjacent pole (Front-Left frame 8), a green vehicle signal is illuminated on the main overhead signal arm controlling ego's direction (Front view, clearly visible in frame 4 as a bright teal/green bulb on the gantry signal head), and another red 'DON'T WALK' hand signal is lit on a pole on the right side (Rear-Right frames 6-8). A dark sedan is stopped alongside on the ego's left (Front-Left), a yellow/orange semi-truck with a silver pickup truck follows behind (Rear), and the 3D box map confirms all these physical agents. Ego motion is steady forward through the intersection, consistent with holding a green light. The Generated output shows a systematic and coordinated SEMANTIC erasure of every illuminated traffic-signal indication while preserving the physical signal housings and poles: (1) the red pedestrian 'DON'T WALK' hand on the Front-Left corner is deleted in Gen frames 5-8 — the signal housing remains but no red bulb is visible; (2) the red vehicle light on the Front-Left pole (GT frame 8) is deleted in Gen, the housing rendered as dark/unlit; (3) the green bulb on the Front overhead signal controlling ego's direction is deleted in Gen frame 4 — the signal head appears as an empty dark housing; (4) the Rear-Right red 'DON'T WALK' hand is deleted in Gen frames 6-8, and in some Gen frames the entire pedestrian pole supporting it is also missing; (5) minor supporting issues — a yellow pedestrian-crossing pole in Rear-Left frame 1 is deleted and the orange Do-Not-Enter sign is simplified. LOGIC component (0.30): the illuminated signals are lit in GT and progressively unlit in Gen, which is a temporal state inconsistency but not severe, since the deletion is uniform across frames rather than flickering. DECISION component (0.35): the generated ego motion still advances through the intersection, which is legal in the GT context because ego has green, so no overt unsafe decision is produced; however, a planner trained on this Gen sequence would receive zero signal-state information at a clearly signalised intersection, which is a latent decision-level risk. Classified as Semantic because the dominant failure is the class/attribute erasure of traffic-light illumination state across multiple views — matching the guideline definition of Semantic-level manipulation of traffic control elements — rather than an explicitly unsafe generated ego behaviour.
